name: Solana de los Barros to La Corte de Peleas
name_original: Solana de los Barros a La Corte de Peleas — circular desde Almendralejo
description: Explore Extremadura's countryside on the moderate 37.6-mile Solana de los Barros a La Corte de Peleas circular mountain bike route.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Almendralejo offers varied terrain across fertile plains, extensive vineyards, and olive groves in the province of Badajoz. The region features rolling hills and dehesa landscapes, providing diverse cycling experiences. Notable natural features include the Alange Reservoir, which is integrated into several routes. This landscape composition makes Almendralejo a suitable destination for mountain bikers seeking different challenges. Best mountain bike trails around Almendralejo The most popular mountain bike trail is Almendralejo — circular por el Embalse de Alange, a 27.5 miles (44.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the Alange Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Solana de los Barros a La Corte de Peleas — circular desde Almendralejo, a moderate 37.6 miles (60.5 km) path. This route connects to nearby towns and traverses varied terrain through the plains. Local mountain bikers also love the Aceuchal desde Almendralejo — circular por Solana de los Barros, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail leading through vineyards and agricultural plains, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Almendralejo is defined by vineyard-covered plains, rolling hills, and routes around the Alange Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Almendralejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Almendralejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Almendralejo?
A: There are 37 mountain bike trails listed on komoot around Almendralejo. The broader region offers over 200 cycling routes, providing a wide range of options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Almendralejo?
A: The terrain around Almendralejo is characterized by fertile plains, extensive vineyards, and olive groves. You'll encounter rolling hills and dehesa landscapes, with many routes traversing sand tracks. Some areas, especially after rain, can become muddy.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Almendralejo?
A: Yes, Almendralejo offers 5 easy mountain bike trails that are suitable for families. These routes typically have gentler gradients and are shorter in length, making them enjoyable for riders of all ages.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Almendralejo?
A: The best times for mountain biking in Almendralejo are generally spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and some areas might become muddy after heavy winter rains, though the region is suitable for year-round cycling with appropriate planning.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that feature circular loops?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Almendralejo are designed as circular loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1274828030/almendralejo-circular-por-el-embalse-de-alange" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendralejo — circular por el Embalse de Alange&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 27.5-mile loop offering scenic views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4082806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyards of Ribera del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2372869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alange Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which sits atop Culebra hill, or ride along the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/742763" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vineyard Road on the Camino de Almendralejo to Torremejía&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Almendralejo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Almendralejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic routes through vineyards, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 3 difficult mountain bike trails around Almendralejo. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Toros de Almendralejo – Feria Castle loop from Almendralejo&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 37.6 miles with significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that connect to nearby towns?
A: Absolutely. Several routes connect Almendralejo to neighboring towns. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1359221696/solana-de-los-barros-a-la-corte-de-peleas-circular-desde-almendralejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solana de los Barros a La Corte de Peleas — circular desde Almendralejo&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.6-mile route that traverses plains and links to nearby settlements.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations for individual trails are not always available, many outdoor trails in Extremadura are suitable for well-behaved dogs on a leash. It's always advisable to check local regulations and ensure your dog is accustomed to longer rides and varied terrain.

Q: What amenities are available along the mountain bike routes, such as cafes or pubs?
A: Most mountain bike routes around Almendralejo traverse rural landscapes. While specific cafes or pubs might not be directly on every trail, the town of Almendralejo and the villages connected by some routes offer various amenities, including places to eat and drink. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks.

Q: Is there public transport access to the mountain bike trails?
A: Public transport options directly to the start of specific mountain bike trails can be limited in rural areas. Almendralejo itself is accessible by regional transport, and from there, you might need to cycle to the trailheads. Planning your route in advance and checking local bus schedules is advisable.

Q: Are there any less crowded mountain bike trails near Almendralejo?
A: To find less crowded trails, consider exploring some of the moderate or difficult routes that venture further from the main town. While popular routes like the Alange Reservoir loop are well-loved, many of the 37 trails offer a chance for a more solitary ride through the extensive vineyards and plains.
tips:
Solana de los Barros is a municipality located in the province of Badajoz, in the autonomous community of Extremadura.
The history of Solana de los Barros dates back to Roman times, with archaeological remains proving the existence of rustic villas in the area. During the Middle Ages, the enclave was occupied by the Knights Templar and later repopulated in the 15th century.
In addition, Solana de los Barros is known for its agricultural production, especially wine and olive oil.

This small town for many years was nothing more than a few families living in the area. However, it began to grow in the 1850s and had more than 1,700 residents by 1920. Currently, there are a little more than 1,200 residents who work mainly in the area's agriculture, which is grapes and olive trees. This means that the area around the town is full of fields and trails that are nice to explore.

On the east side of Almendralejo, near the train station, you reach the impressive Plaza de Toros and Parque de la Piedad and the monumental Ermita de Nuestra Señora de la Piedad. A great meeting point before the start of the adventure or a place to rest at the end.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain typical of the Extremadura region. You can expect a mix of surfaces, including significant sections of loose ground, some asphalt, and paved paths. This diversity adds to the challenge and experience of the ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the natural beauty of Extremadura, with views of the countryside. The region is known for its diverse landscapes, including forests of chestnut, oak, and holm oaks. Solana de los Barros itself is situated on a hill overlooking the Guadajira River, offering scenic vistas.

faqs.question: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route combines natural cycling with points of historical interest. You'll pass by the 16th-century Church of Solana de los Barros, and the settlement of Corte de Peleas. Near Almendralejo, you can also see the Plaza de Toros de Almendralejo. Solana de los Barros also has evidence of a significant Chalcolithic site and Roman vestiges nearby.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is officially classified as 'moderate'. While it requires good physical fitness, the technical demands can vary. Shorter moderate routes might be accessible to most, but for a 60 km trail like this, some prior mountain biking experience and endurance would be beneficial.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Extremadura offers varied terrain suitable for outdoor sports. To avoid extreme heat, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for mountain biking in this region, offering milder temperatures and beautiful natural colors.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Since the route is circular and starts from Almendralejo, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of Almendralejo. Specific parking areas for trail access are not detailed, but public parking in the town would be your starting point.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not provided, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas in Spain, especially if kept under control. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride this mountain bike route. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Does this mountain bike route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de Lobón, Paseo Fluvial, Camino de Husero, and Camino Sancho, among others, as you navigate the circular route.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Almendralejo, a larger town, you'll find various amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options there. The route also passes through settlements like Corte de Peleas, where you might find local services.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length (over 60 km), you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing for the weather. A helmet is essential, and navigation tools like a GPS device or the komoot app are highly recommended.
